text: The SGSPA is devoted to delivering an exceptional suite of services to our community, supporting academic, professional, and personal ambitions. By promoting a vibrant and thriving graduate and postdoctoral community, enhancing the graduate and postdoctoral experience, and constantly developing new research and learning opportunities, we collectively work to strengthen Queen’s reputation as a trailblazing research institution—one with a powerful vision of an equitable, just, and sustainable society predicated on the values of care, compassion, creativity, and curiosity.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Dr. Fahim Quadir serves as Vice-Provost and Dean of the School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs
metadata.name: Dr. Fahim Quadir

text: Our mission at the School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs is to create a learning and research culture that inspires graduate students and postdoctoral fellows to lead the way towards a better future for our global community. Learning at Queen’s is inspired by an inclusive and collaborative community where we prioritize wellbeing for academic and professional success. Through our local and global reach, we connect our students and fellows with diverse communities to engage in collaborative knowledge creation and real-world scholarship.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Queen’s graduate students and postdoctoral fellows lead the way towards an equitable, just, and sustainable society through care, compassion, creativity, and curiosity. Our students and fellows apply advanced skills and capacities to explore questions that expand knowledge, promote wellbeing, and serve the benefit of our common good.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Through our work at the School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs, and through graduate education and postdoctoral research we are committed to the following values:

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Care: Through an ethic of care and integrity, we are committed to advancing sustainability and to positive social change within our local and global communities.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Compassion: Through compassion and respect, we strive towards a culture of inclusion and belonging with a commitment to reducing systemic barriers and inequities.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Creativity: Through creativity and innovation, we work collaboratively and with our communities to create and express new knowledge that has value and impact.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Curiosity: Through curiosity, we pursue important questions through diverse perspectives, traditions, and forms of knowledge.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: The School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs has engaged in an extensive community consultation process to identify a strategic action plan and set of priorities. Strategic priorities are articulated within the Working Group Report on Graduate Student Success, which contains 35 recommendations with targeted actions and related timelines.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: As one of the leading universities in Canada, Queen’s offers a vibrant and supportive community for graduate students and postdoctoral fellows to conduct their research and training. Queen's professional programs and research-centred degrees reflect the University’s excellence as a space for forward thinkers. Our nationally and internationally recognized faculty provide effective supervision for students and fellows working in state-of-the-art interdisciplinary facilities, engaging with the United Nations’ Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: It is an exciting time to join the SGSPA at Queen’s because the University, through its new Strategic Research Plan, affirms a renewed focus on teaching and research and highlights the significant role played by students and fellows.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Gordon Hall, Member of U15, $28 Million in Funding for Graduate Students Each Year, 22 Research Centres & Institutes, 51 Canada Research Chairs, Ranks #1 in Canada: 2025 Times Higher Education Sustainability Impact Rankings.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Graduate students and postdoctoral fellows have many opportunities to share their work and receive training through the Professional Development series, 3 Minute Thesis competition, Grad Chat, PhD Community Initiative, and more. Health and wellbeing resources are available to our graduate students and postdocs, enabling and promoting academic, professional, and personal success. A federal and provincial investment of $36 million provided a Student Wellness and Innovation Centre to help put ideas in motion. The SGSPA ensures your time at Queen’s is as productive and rewarding as possible.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: The beautiful city of Kingston surrounds the University’s diverse and inclusive campus. Located in eastern Ontario, Kingston is close to Toronto, Ottawa, and Montreal, and is a perfect city to enjoy Lake Ontario, the St. Lawrence River (Thousand Islands), and the Rideau Canal (a UNESCO World Heritage site). With restaurants, museums, galleries, performance venues, music festivals, green spaces and historical sites, Kingston is culturally rich and ideal for graduate students and postdocs, including those with families. Queen’s own Agnes Etherington Art Centre and Isabel Bader Centre for the Performing Arts demonstrate the University’s commitment to the arts and offer programs for families.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: The Graduate Studies Executive Council (GSEC) ensures that Faculty Graduate Councils and Committees establish and follow appropriate procedures related to graduate programs. It reviews and revises regulations concerning admissions standards, degree requirements, student progress, completion of degree requirements, and appeals. GSEC refers new programs for consideration, reviews program proposals for recommendation to Senate, appoints sub-committees as needed, advises the Dean or Associate Deans, considers matters referred by Faculty Councils, Standing Committees, or Senate, and reviews and approves decisions made at the Faculty level.

metadata.faculty: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: The Graduate Studies Executive Council calls and organizes a Forum of all members of the SGSPA at least twice per academic session, moderated by the Dean of the SGSPA. Decisions made by GSEC are recommended or provided as information to Senate and its subcommittees, as required by SGSPA or University regulations, and to Faculty Graduate Councils and Committees.

metadata.faculty: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Membership of the Graduate Studies Executive Council includes the Vice-Provost and Dean of the School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs (Chair), Associate Deans of SGSPA, the Senior Officer of SGSPA, Associate Deans of Graduate Studies from Faculties and School, Chairs of Faculty Graduate Councils, the Society of Graduate and Professional Students (SGPS) President or delegate, and a graduate student Senator.

metadata.faculty: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: The Faculty of Arts and Science Graduate Council establishes procedures governing graduate programs within the Faculty. Its manual outlines policies and appendices related to thesis examination committees for master’s students, promotion to doctoral programs without completion of a master’s degree, and processes for graduate course additions, revisions, deletions, and degree plan revisions.

metadata.program_name: Faculty of Arts and Science Graduate Council

text: The Smith Engineering Graduate Council oversees graduate academic policies within Smith Engineering at Queen’s University. Its manual and appendices outline procedures for nominating council student members, introducing new graduate courses, revising existing courses, deleting courses, and revising graduate degree plans.

The Faculty of Health Sciences Graduate Council governs graduate academic matters within the Faculty of Health Sciences. Its manual details policies and procedures for new graduate courses, course revisions and deletions, and degree program revisions.

metadata.program_name: Faculty of Health Sciences Graduate Council

text: The Faculty of Law Graduate Committee establishes rules and procedures for graduate studies in Law. Its manual and appendices include processes for graduate course additions, revisions, deletions, and graduate program revisions.

The Graduate Studies and Research Committee in Education defines the structure, roles, and responsibilities for graduate studies in Education. Its documentation includes policies for graduate course revisions, additions, deletions, degree program revisions, and promotion to the doctoral program without completion of a master’s degree.

metadata.program_name: Graduate Studies and Research Committee in Education

text: The Graduate Committee for Business defines the structure, roles, and responsibilities of graduate programs and outlines procedures for graduate courses revisions, additions or deletions, revising graduate degree programs, and granting advanced standing for prior coursework.
